title: Tang Yuan Glutinous Rice Balls

seo.title: How to Make Tang Yuan Glutinous Rice Balls — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Tang Yuan Glutinous Rice Balls. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Tang Yuan Glutinous Rice Balls recipe
authentic Chinese Tang Yuan Glutinous Rice Balls

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Tang Yuan Glutinous Rice Balls recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Tang Yuan Glutinous Rice Balls — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Tang Yuan are soft, chewy glutinous rice balls, often served in a sweet ginger soup. This traditional Chinese dessert symbolizes togetherness and completeness, making it a beloved dish for festivals and family gatherings. Enjoy these delightful spheres, often filled with sweet black sesame paste, for a truly comforting treat.

ingredients.notes: or to taste, for the soup

instructions.title: Prepare the Sesame Filling
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine the ground black sesame seeds, granulated sugar, and softened lard or vegetable oil. Mix well until a thick paste forms. Divide the paste into small balls, about 1 teaspoon each. Place them on a plate and chill in the refrigerator for 10 minutes to firm up.

instructions.title: Make the Glutinous Rice Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, gradually add the warm water to the glutinous rice flour while mixing with your hands. Knead the mixture until it forms a smooth, soft, and pliable dough. It should not be too sticky or too dry. Adjust water or flour as needed.

instructions.title: Form the Tang Yuan Balls
instructions.text: Take a small piece of dough, about 1 tablespoon, and flatten it into a disc in your palm. Place one chilled sesame filling ball in the center of the dough disc. Carefully wrap the dough around the filling, pinching the edges to seal completely. Roll it gently between your palms to form a smooth, round ball. Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.

instructions.title: Cook the Tang Yuan
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Gently drop the Tang Yuan balls into the boiling water, stirring lightly to prevent them from sticking to the bottom. Once they float to the surface, reduce the heat to medium-low and continue to cook for another 2-3 minutes until they are thoroughly cooked through and appear translucent.

instructions.title: Prepare the Ginger Soup
instructions.text: While the Tang Yuan are cooking, in a separate small saucepan, combine 6 cups of water, rock sugar, and ginger slices. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the rock sugar to dissolve. Adjust sweetness to your preference.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Once the Tang Yuan are cooked, use a slotted spoon to transfer them directly into bowls of the hot ginger soup. Serve immediately while warm. For an extra touch, you can garnish with a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.

tips: Use warm water when kneading the glutinous rice flour to achieve a more pliable and easier-to-work-with dough, which helps prevent cracking when forming the balls.

Adjust the amount of sugar in both the sesame filling and the ginger soup according to your personal sweetness preference. You can also experiment with other fillings like peanut paste or red bean paste.

Tang Yuan are best served immediately after cooking. If preparing ahead, you can freeze uncooked Tang Yuan on a tray until solid, then transfer to a freezer bag for up to one month. Cook directly from frozen in boiling water.
article: Tang Yuan Glutinous Rice Balls is a beloved Chinese Desserts dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
